name: Sun Country Home Solutions Branch
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention counseling, and financial guidance to help renters and homeowners achieve housing stability across rural Utah.
description_long:
Sun Country Home Solutions (formerly NeighborWorks Mountain Country Home Solutions) is a 25+ year old nonprofit organization focused on improving neighborhoods and increasing homeownership rates across rural Utah. The organization addresses Utah's housing affordability challenges through education, counseling, and direct affordable housing development in communities including Richfield, Kanab, Springdale, Escalante, Enoch, Orem, and Tropic. They operate as a HUD-certified counseling provider, meaning their advisors meet federal standards for housing counseling expertise and ethical practice.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free counseling services across three life stages: homebuyer education and financial counseling for new buyers; rental counseling and application support for renters; and foreclosure prevention, reverse mortgage counseling, and post-purchase support for existing homeowners. All counseling programs are structured to help clients make informed decisions about their housing situation, whether that means preparing for homeownership, preventing loss of a home, or understanding reverse mortgage options. They combine direct financial education with affordable housing development projects, functioning as both a counseling provider and a housing developer.

Sun Country Home Solutions distinguishes itself through deep rural community integration, having developed multiple housing projects across sparsely-served counties and built partnerships with local government officials, financial institutions, and employers. Their work directly impacts workforce housing availability by helping young families afford homes in rural areas. The organization explicitly emphasizes neighborhood impact, noting that homeownership rates affect neighborhood desirability and community stability. They operate with endorsements from county commissioners and community bankers who serve on their board.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are concentrated in rural southern and central Utah (Garfield, Kane, Rich, Daggett counties and surrounding areas), making them inaccessible to consumers outside these regions. While they offer free counseling across multiple housing situations, they function as a regional nonprofit rather than a scalable national provider. For rural Utah residents facing housing challenges, this is a high-credibility, locally-embedded resource; for others, it offers no service.
pros: HUD-certified counselors ensure advice meets federal standards for housing counseling quality and ethics
Completely free counseling services across homebuyer education, financial planning, foreclosure prevention, and reverse mortgage guidance
25+ years of operational history and deep integration with rural Utah communities, county government, and local financial institutions
Comprehensive life-stage approach supporting renters transitioning to buyers, current homeowners avoiding foreclosure, and seniors evaluating reverse mortgages
Direct affordable housing development through multiple completed projects across 8+ communities, not just abstract counseling
Board representation from local bankers and county officials creates accountability and financial sector alignment
Post-purchase counseling and reverse mortgage counseling offered, addressing long-term homeowner challenges beyond initial purchase
cons: Geographic limitation: services only available in rural southern and central Utah counties, excluding most of the state and the nation
No evidence of online counseling options or remote service delivery; appears to require in-person engagement in service area
Website does not specify counselor availability, appointment wait times, or service volume capacity
Limited information about specific program outcomes or success metrics for homebuyer conversion or foreclosure prevention
No mention of services in Spanish or other languages despite serving rural areas with diverse workforce populations
best_for: Prospective first-time homebuyers in rural southern Utah wanting free pre-purchase education and financial counseling
Homeowners facing foreclosure in Garfield, Kane, Rich, or Daggett counties seeking loss-mitigation counseling
Renters in the service area preparing to transition to homeownership with financial counseling support
Seniors in rural Utah communities evaluating reverse mortgage options with HUD-certified guidance
diagnosis: Sun Country Home Solutions is the right choice for rural Utah residents (primarily south-central Utah counties) seeking free, HUD-certified housing counseling across any stage of the homeownership journey—from renting to buying to staying in a home during financial hardship. The critical caveat is geographic restriction: this organization serves only rural southern/central Utah and cannot help consumers outside this region, making it unsuitable for the vast majority of the country.
services: HUD-certified homebuyer education workshops for new buyers
Financial and budget counseling for new buyers and renters
Rental counseling and rental application assistance
Foreclosure prevention counseling for at-risk homeowners
Affordable housing development and project management
Community partnership and financial partner coordination
Workforce housing solutions for rural employers and communities
Neighborhood improvement initiatives focused on homeownership
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 382 S. Bluff St. #150, Saint George, UT 84770
